channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Is there a right time for a Panenka - as Brentford pay the price?
item.description: Brentford are beaten by West Ham on penalties as Dango Ouattara is left to rue a missed Panenka. Is there ever a right rime to take one?

item.title: Man City host Liverpool, Port Vale face Chelsea - full FA Cup draw
item.description: League One Port Vale's reward for beating Sunderland in the FA Cup is a quarter-final tie away to Chelsea, while Manchester City will host Liverpool in a heavyweight showdown.

item.title: Alisson injury not 'a big thing' despite missing Galatasaray
item.description: Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson is ruled out of the first leg of their Champions League tie against Galatasaray through injury - but could return to face Tottenham at the weekend.

item.title: Should lower-ranked teams always play at home in the FA Cup?
item.description: After some superb FA Cup fifth-round ties, BBC Sport looks at whether lower-ranked teams should always get to play at home.

item.title: Scholes 'did not intend to be offensive' to Carrick
item.description: Paul Scholes says an Instagram story post about Manchester United interim head coach Michael Carrick was "in no way intended to be offensive" towards his former team-mate.

item.title: Premier League survival is Spurs' priority - Tudor
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur will prioritise earning Premier League survival over Champions League progress, says interim manager Igor Tudor.

item.title: MLS hands life bans to Yeboah & Jones for betting offences
item.description: Major League Soccer gives lifetime bans to midfielder Derrick Jones and winger Yaw Yeboah for betting offences.

item.title: Juve want Tottenham's Spence - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Tottenham's Djed Spence a target for Juventus, Real Madrid put Mauricio Pochettino on managerial shortlist, Liverpool consider move for French winger Moussa Diaby, plus more.

item.title: Regulator proposals put diversity on subs bench - Kick It Out
item.description: The head of anti-discrimination body Kick It Out says that initial proposals by English football's independent regulator "put equality, diversity and inclusion on the subs bench".

item.title: Is time-wasting in the Premier League getting worse?
item.description: With time-wasting seemingly on the rise in the Premier League, how bad is it getting, are fans receiving value for money and what is being done about it?

item.title: Ouattara's Panenka saved as Brentford lose to West Ham in penalty shootout
item.description: Dango Ouattara's Panenka is saved by Alphonso Areola as Brentford lose in a penalty shootout in their FA Cup fifth round tie against West Ham.

item.title: West Ham into quarters after beating Brentford on penalties
item.description: Watch highlights as West Ham reach the quarter-finals of the FA Cup for the first time in 10 years by defeating London rivals Brentford 5-3 on penalties.

item.title: Two brilliant headers and a 'Shearer-style' celebration in Port Vale win
item.description: Watch as Ellen White and Glenn Murray dissect two brilliant headers in Port Vale's 1-0 FA Cup win over Sunderland, including an Alan Shearer-inspired celebration from Vale hero Ben Waine.

item.title: 'Wrong reason, right decision' - Why referee blew whistle to deny Fulham goal
item.description: Match of the Day's Ellen White and Glenn Murray analyse why referee Jarred Gillett blew his whistle to deny Fulham a goal in their 1-0 FA Cup defeat to Southampton.

item.title: Mourinho sent off after late Benfica equaliser
item.description: Benfica manager Jose Mourinho is shown a red card following an altercation between the benches after his side score a late equaliser against his former club FC Porto.

item.title: Watch Celtic beat Rangers on penalties to reach Scottish Cup semis
item.description: Watch highlights as Celtic beat Rangers on penalties at Ibrox to reach the Scottish Cup semi-finals.

item.title: Old Firm chaos: What happened? Who said what? What now?
item.description: After ugly scenes at Sunday's Old Firm derby, what do we know about the fan clashes and what could happen next?

item.title: I want my team to be talked about like Asprilla and Gillespie - Howe
item.description: Head coach Eddie Howe challenges his Newcastle United players to seize the moment and follow in the footsteps of the side who defeated Barcelona in 1997.

item.title: 23 players sent off after mass brawl in Brazil - but it's not a world record
item.description: "It's regrettable," says former Brazil forward Hulk after becoming one of 23 players are sent off following a mass brawl between Cruzeiro and Atletico Mineiro.

item.title: Will Wolves pull off the Premier League's greatest escape?
item.description: Wolves are bottom of the Premier League, 12 points off safety. But two wins in a row have raised faint hopes that they could survive relegation.

item.title: Man City's Rodri fined £80,000 for ref comments
item.description: Manchester City midfielder Rodri is fined for saying the referee was not "neutral" in last month's Premier League draw at Tottenham.

item.title: Five Iranian footballers granted Australian visas after anthem protest
item.description: Concern has grown for team after one critic called them "wartime traitors" for failing to salute during the Iranian anthem.

item.title: Nolan sacked as Northampton boss after poor run
item.description: Northampton Town sack manager Kevin Nolan after a run of one win in 16 games with the club in the League One relegation places.

item.title: Quiz: How much do you remember from this weekend's FA Cup action?
item.description: Seven of the eight FA Cup fifth-round ties have been played, but were you paying attention? Take on our quiz.

item.title: 'Disciplined, brave, relentless' - new NI boss McArdle lays out plans
item.description: Michael McArdle, the Scottish FA's head of elite women's football and former interim head coach of Scotland, is appointed Northern Ireland manager.

item.title: Millwall fined for abusive disability chanting
item.description: Millwall are fined £45,000 by the FA for abusive chanting with a reference to disability during their Carabao Cup tie with Crystal Palace.

item.title: Iraq coach wants World Cup play-off to be postponed
item.description: Iraq head coach Graham Arnold wants Iraq's World Cup play-off decider to be rescheduled to the week before the tournament to give his side a fair chance of qualifying.

item.title: Watch: Saints & Hamilton win on the road as Sons stun Spartans
item.description: Watch the best of the action from the weekend's action in the Scottish Championship, League 1 and League 2.

item.title: League One side Port Vale stun Sunderland in FA Cup upset
item.description: League One side Port Vale knock out Premier League team Sunderland to reach the FA Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954.

item.title: Southampton knock out Fulham with stoppage-time penalty
item.description: Southampton's Ross Stewart scores a penalty in stoppage time against Fulham which sends the Championship side to the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Leeds ease past Norwich to reach FA Cup quarter-finals
item.description: Leeds reach the FA Cup quarter-finals for the first time in over 20 years, after comfortably beating Championship side Norwich 3-0 at Elland Road.

item.title: Watch Rangers and Celtic fans invade pitch after cup tie
item.description: Rangers and Celtic fans run on to the Ibrox pitch after the visitors win on penalties in the Scottish Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: What now for 'atrocious' and 'embarrassing' Aberdeen?
item.description: What now for Aberdeen after the holders were dumped out of the Scottish Cup by second-tier Dunfermline Athletic?

item.title: Will Celtic's 'signing of season' somehow salvage silverware?
item.description: One man stands apart as Celtic's talisman this season - and he's not on the pitch.

item.title: Clubs complain to X about 'sickening' Grok posts
item.description: The UK government says it is "sickening and irresponsible" that X's AI tool Grok generated explicit posts about the Hillsborough and Heysel disasters, the death of Diogo Jota and the Munich air disaster.

item.title: Rooney praise an 'incredible tribute' for Wrexham
item.description: Phil Parkinson says Wayne Rooney's praise for Wrexham's FA Cup display against Chelsea is an "incredible tribute" for the club.

item.title: Falkirk & Dunfermline to meet in semi-final derby
item.description: Falkirk and Dunfermline Athletic will meet in a Scottish Cup semi-final derby at Hampden next month, while Celtic will face St Mirren in a repeat of this season's League Cup final.

item.title: Chelsea overcome Wrexham in extra-time FA Cup thriller
item.description: Chelsea come from behind twice to scupper 10-man Wrexham's hopes of a giant-killing in a thrilling FA Cup fifth-round tie.

item.title: Eze stunner spares Arsenal from Mansfield upset
item.description: Arsenal's Eberechi Eze comes off the bench to score the winning goal against League One side Mansfield, sending the Gunners through to the FA Cup quarter-finals.

item.title: Scotland fire seven past Luxembourg in World Cup qualifying
item.description: Watch as Scotland beat Luxembourg 7-0 in their Women's World Cup qualifier at Hampden Park to go top of Group B4 ahead of Belgium on goal difference.

item.title: Smith fires Wrexham in front against Chelsea
item.description: Wrexham's Sam Smith races past the Chelsea defence to put his side ahead in their FA Cup fifth-round tie.

item.title: Wrexham owners understand the magic of the FA Cup
item.description: Wrexham's community director Humphrey Ker explains how club owners Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney bought into the "magic of the FA Cup".

item.title: Pressure mounts on Tudor as Tottenham lose at home to Palace
item.description: Tottenham stay just one point above the Premier League relegation zone following a 3-1 home defeat by Crystal Palace, having had to play more than 45 minutes with 10 men after Micky van de Ven was sent off.

item.title: Ten-man Newcastle score late winner to beat Man Utd
item.description: Substitute William Osula scores a stoppage-time winner to help 10-man Newcastle beat Manchester United 2-1 at St James' Park, as Michael Carrick suffers his first defeat as Red Devils boss.

item.title: Man City suffer title race setback as stubborn Forest secure vital draw
item.description: Manchester City's hopes of winning the Premier League suffer a setback as they are held at home by strugglers Nottingham Forest, as the away side come from behind twice to grab a 2-2 draw at Etihad Stadium.

item.title: Joao Pedro hat-trick as Chelsea come from behind to beat Villa
item.description: Joao Pedro scores a hat-trick as Chelsea come from a goal down to secure an impressive win against Aston Villa, boosting their hopes of qualifying for next season's Champions League.

item.title: Saka scores only goal as leaders Arsenal edge past Brighton
item.description: Bukayo Saka scores the only goal as Arsenal beat Brighton 1-0 at the Amex to move seven points clear at the top of the Premier League.

item.title: Bottom of the league, magic in the cup - Port Vale's 'incredible' run
item.description: Port Vale are bottom of League One, but they have reached the FA Cup quarter-finals for only the second time in their history.

item.title: 'Disgraceful scenes as toxicity spills over in Old Firm cup tie'
item.description: As if the ugliness of the 120 minutes wasn't enough there was disgrace as Rangers and Celtic fans invaded the pitch at Ibrox, writes Tom English.

item.title: The anatomy of a corner-kick: how set-pieces reshaped the Premier League
item.description: As corner kicks continue to cause chaos in the Premier League, BBC Sport take a closer at why they are becoming so hard to defend.

item.title: 'I'll go on holiday' - Guardiola fumes as touchline ban looms
item.description: Manchester City boss Pep Guardiola is facing a two-game touchline ban after being booked for the sixth time this season during Saturday's FA Cup win at Newcastle.

item.title: 'When you're with me, you're not bored' - Malard bringing sunshine to Man Utd
item.description: BBC Sport meets Manchester United and France forward Melvine Malard to discover her story.

item.title: Liverpool cruise past Wolves to seal quarter-final spot
item.description: Liverpool secure a 3–1 victory over Wolverhampton Wanderers at Molineux to advance to the quarter-finals of the FA Cup.

item.title: Early goals send Falkirk to Scottish Cup semi-finals
item.description: Two early goals help Falkirk make it by Dundee United to reach the Scottish Cup semi-finals.

item.title: Bayern hammer Monchengladbach to move 14 points clear
item.description: Bayern Munich win their fifth match in a row with a 4-1 win over Borussia Monchengladbach, to move to 14 points clear at the top of the Bundesliga table.

item.title: 'As pure as you can hit it!' Robertson fires Liverpool into lead
item.description: Liverpool left-back Andrew Robertson scores a superb opening goal for his side in their FA Cup fifth round tie against Wolves at Molineux.

item.title: 'We don't know what is a foul' - Nuno wants set-piece review
item.description: With increased attention on grappling at corner kicks in the Premier League, West Ham head coach Nuno Espirito Santo wants officials to explain what is and is not a foul.

item.title: Who is to blame for Spurs' chaotic descent towards relegation?
item.description: BBC Sport's chief football writer Phil McNulty examines who - or what - is to blame for Spurs' crisis as they fight to avoid relegation.

item.title: 'Howard Webb, where are you?' - How we can stop VAR ruining football
item.description: In his BBC Sport column, former Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains how VAR has become a monster, and why people in the game should be asked how to fix it.

item.title: Why are celebrities buying stakes in football clubs?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the growing trend for big household names to invest in clubs in the lower leagues of English football.

item.title: Handball, offside, VAR - is football too complicated?
item.description: From handball, to offside and VAR, it has become difficult for supporters to follow what is going on with football.

item.title: From PE teacher to Premier League boss for Boro's Hellberg?
item.description: Middlesbrough head coach Kim Hellberg talks to BBC Radio Tees about his previous career and life in Sweden.

item.title: Haaland? Kane? R9? Ranking the best centre-forwards this century
item.description: Who are the best centre-forwards of the 21st Century? We've had a go at trying to whittle down a pretty formidable list.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: What is St Totteringham's Day and when could it happen?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what St Totteringham's Day is and what its origins are

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: Where do iconic football moves originate from?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain where the names of iconic football moves come from.

item.title: Why is there a four-week break in the WSL?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at why there is a second hiatus in the Women's Super League so soon after the winter break.

item.title: Two charged after brick thrown at supporters' bus
item.description: It follows an incident after Queen of the South's home clash with Stenhousemuir in Dumfries.

item.title: Injured Kane's ever-present league record to end
item.description: Bayern Munich's Harry Kane is ruled out of Friday's game against Borussia Monchengladbach, ending his ever-present record in the Bundesliga this season.

item.title: Brazil's Rodrygo to miss World Cup with ACL injury
item.description: Brazil's Real Madrid forward Rodrygo calls it "one of the worst days of my life" after he is ruled out for the rest of the season and 2026 World Cup with a serious knee injury.

item.title: Galatasaray fans banned from Liverpool second leg
item.description: Galatasaray fans are banned from the club's Champions League visit to Liverpool after disturbances during their game with Juventus in the previous round.

item.title: Mbappe has knee sprain with no surgery planned
item.description: Real Madrid striker Kylian Mbappe is diagnosed with a left knee sprain and will not have surgery at this stage.

item.title: Watford Women set for Vicarage Road return in title push
item.description: Watford Women are to play their final two home games of the season at Vicarage Road after a 14-0 win put them in pole position to win promotion to WSL 2.

item.title: 'Incredible' Bronze remains England's gold standard
item.description: Lucy Bronze made her 145th senior appearance for England on Saturday. Few have been better performances.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
